hyperedges.label: Feature Spec Review and Triage Loop

nodes.label: @lousy-agents/cli
nodes.rationale: The CLI is the primary entry point and first step in the recommended adoption flow.

nodes.label: @lousy-agents/mcp
nodes.rationale: The MCP package is optional and used only when deeper AI assistant integration is needed.

nodes.label: @lousy-agents/agent-shell
nodes.rationale: agent-shell is positioned as a companion telemetry tool rather than the default starting package.

nodes.label: Spec-Driven Development
nodes.rationale: The project argues that agents perform better when given explicit specs before implementation.

nodes.label: Non-Interactive Mode
nodes.rationale: Non-interactive flags are emphasized for CI/CD and automation use cases.

nodes.label: TDD Required
nodes.rationale: Gemini guidance mandates research, failing test, minimal implementation, and validation as the required workflow for code changes.

nodes.label: Beads Task Tracking
nodes.rationale: Gemini instructions forbid ad-hoc task tracking and require Beads as the authoritative task system.

nodes.label: Repository Lesson Storage
nodes.rationale: Lessons are stored as committed markdown files under .lousy-agents/lessons so Git remains the audit trail and no database is needed.

nodes.label: Passive Runtime
nodes.rationale: The runtime reads and injects lessons but never authors them, keeping lesson creation in normal agent write/edit flows.

nodes.label: Context Injection Hook
nodes.rationale: Edit and Write hooks invoke `lousy-agents context` with validated hook input so file paths are extracted internally instead of interpolated into shell commands.

nodes.label: Lesson Matching Rules
nodes.rationale: Matching uses path globs, tag matches, and literal substring search only, avoiding regex on untrusted content, embeddings, and model calls in PreToolUse.

nodes.label: Error Behavior Matrix
nodes.rationale: v1 explicitly mixes fail-open and fail-closed behavior to keep runtime assistance resilient while making authoring and installation failures visible.

nodes.label: TDD Required
nodes.rationale: Claude instructions require the full TDD loop and final validation with `mise run ci` before a task can be considered done.

nodes.label: Clean Architecture
nodes.rationale: Source changes under src/ must follow inward-pointing Clean Architecture boundaries.

nodes.label: Beads Task Tracking
nodes.rationale: Claude instructions mandate Beads for all project task tracking and prohibit ad-hoc or session-local substitutes.

nodes.label: GitHub Security Advisories Reporting
nodes.rationale: Vulnerabilities must be reported privately through GitHub Security Advisories instead of public issues.

nodes.label: Reviewdog Integration
nodes.rationale: The action installs reviewdog, emits rdjsonl from the packaged Node entrypoint, and pipes results into reviewdog reporters for inline GitHub feedback.

nodes.label: Custom Copilot Agents
nodes.rationale: The `new` command scaffolds repository-scoped custom agents with frontmatter, responsibilities, guidelines, and examples.

nodes.label: Agent Skills
nodes.rationale: The `new skill` subcommand scaffolds reusable Copilot skills with trigger conditions, instructions, and examples.

nodes.label: Instruction Quality Analysis
nodes.rationale: The lint command evaluates feedback loop documentation across structural context, execution clarity, and loop completeness.

nodes.label: Feedback Loop Tools
nodes.rationale: The MCP server exposes discovery and validation tools that map scripts and workflow commands into SDLC feedback loops.

nodes.label: Claude Code Web Setup
nodes.rationale: The MCP server can generate or update `.claude/settings.json` and `CLAUDE.md` based on detected runtime and dependency setup.

nodes.label: Scaffolded Project Kinds
nodes.rationale: The init command scaffolds opinionated webapp, API, and CLI project types with testing, linting, instructions, and environment setup.

nodes.label: Copilot PR Review Ruleset
nodes.rationale: copilot-setup can create a PR review ruleset and conditionally add code scanning rules when GitHub Advanced Security is enabled.

nodes.label: ActionInputs
nodes.rationale: Validated action inputs normalize environment variables into a typed structure shared between input parsing and lint orchestration.

nodes.label: Lousy Agents
nodes.rationale: Scaffolding tool for AI-agent workflows that supports simple, configurable, shareable patterns for high-velocity/vibe coding experiments.

nodes.label: High-Velocity Decision Making
nodes.rationale: The product optimizes for two-way-door experiments and emergent agentic components rather than heavyweight upfront design.

nodes.label: c12 Configuration Engine
nodes.rationale: Shareable team/project configuration is a core product characteristic.

nodes.label: Shared Instruction Architecture
nodes.rationale: The repository keeps domain instructions in .github/instructions as a shared source of truth for Copilot and Claude Code.

nodes.label: Mise Validation Suite
nodes.rationale: Mise centralizes tool/runtime execution and validation commands across local and CI environments.

nodes.label: TDD Required Workflow
nodes.rationale: All code changes must proceed through research, failing test, minimal implementation, pass, refactor, and full validation.

nodes.label: TypeScript and Zod Runtime Validation
nodes.rationale: External data must be parsed with Zod and fetch responses must check response.ok to avoid unsafe type assertions.

nodes.label: Beads Task Tracking
nodes.rationale: Project work tracking must remain visible to all agents via bd rather than session-local task lists.

nodes.label: Reviewer Handoff Protocol
nodes.rationale: Code changes require local validation plus explicit security and architecture review invocation before completion.

nodes.label: Clean Architecture Dependency Rule
nodes.rationale: Dependencies point inward only to keep business rules independent from frameworks, adapters, and infrastructure.

nodes.label: Entities Layer
nodes.rationale: Entities hold business rules, avoid framework/global dependencies, and receive generated IDs/timestamps from outer layers.

nodes.label: Use Cases Layer
nodes.rationale: Use cases define DTOs and ports and orchestrate application rules without importing concrete implementations.

nodes.label: Adapters Layer
nodes.rationale: Adapters convert interfaces and may use frameworks, but must not own business decisions.

nodes.label: Infrastructure Composition Root
nodes.rationale: Composition roots wire concrete dependencies and may import from all layers.

nodes.label: Constructor Injection
nodes.rationale: Explicit dependency injection improves testability and avoids hidden framework/global coupling.

nodes.label: Factory Function Adapters
nodes.rationale: Simple adapters may use factories that return port implementations while preserving explicit dependencies.

nodes.label: Arrange-Act-Assert Tests
nodes.rationale: Tests are executable documentation that describe behavior and isolate setup, action, and assertions.

nodes.label: Chance MSW Vitest Stack
nodes.rationale: Random-but-readable fixtures, MSW HTTP mocking, and Vitest are mandatory testing patterns.

nodes.label: Dependency Injection for Testing
nodes.rationale: Production code must not expose test-only mutation hooks; dependencies should flow through public APIs.

nodes.label: GitHub Actions Workflow Pinning
nodes.rationale: Third-party actions must be pinned to exact SHAs with version comments for reproducibility and supply-chain safety.

nodes.label: Workflow Validation Commands
nodes.rationale: Workflow changes must be validated with linting and must include test/lint jobs.

nodes.label: EARS Requirement Syntax
nodes.rationale: EARS makes acceptance criteria precise by tying responses to event, state, option, and error conditions.

nodes.label: Feature Value Assessment
nodes.rationale: Every feature should explicitly state commercial, future, customer, market, or efficiency value.

nodes.label: Persona Coverage
nodes.rationale: Specs must name impacted personas and describe impact rather than designing for an abstract user.

nodes.label: Agent-Sized Task Breakdown
nodes.rationale: Tasks should be small, sequenced, verifiable units that a coding agent can complete in one session.

nodes.label: Copilot Feature To Spec Issue Template
nodes.norm_label: copilot feature to spec issue template

nodes.label: Orient Create Validate Phases
nodes.rationale: The issue template forces agents to orient on instructions, create specs in order, and validate through review/triage loops.

nodes.label: Conversational Spec Planning Flow
nodes.rationale: The planner gathers context, acceptance criteria, and clarifications before generating EARS requirements, diagrams, and tasks.

nodes.label: Behavioral Contract Review
nodes.rationale: Custom agents are reviewed as behavioral contracts vulnerable to drift, scope violations, and structural failures.

nodes.label: Agent Archetype Dimensions
nodes.rationale: Agent reliability is evaluated by archetype-specific dimensions for evaluator, workflow, conversational, and reviewer agents.

nodes.label: Agent Review Priority Mapping
nodes.rationale: PASS/WARN/FAIL findings map deterministically to P0-P3 priorities to avoid inconsistent severity selection.

nodes.label: Adversarial Simulation Review
nodes.rationale: The reviewer treats code as an attack surface and checks concrete failure modes such as traversal, injection, symlink attacks, and resource exhaustion.

nodes.label: Negative Findings Only Review Output
nodes.rationale: The reviewer is designed to maximize signal by reporting only concrete defects with severity, evil path, doc reference, and fix.

nodes.label: Verify Review Claims Before Acting
nodes.rationale: Automated review comments are hypotheses; claims must be verified against code, tests, and deliberate design choices before action.

nodes.label: PR Review Resolution Workflow
nodes.rationale: Fixes are prioritized, tested, validated, replied to in-thread, and resolved through GitHub review threads.

nodes.label: Dynamic Action Version Resolution
nodes.rationale: Delegates GitHub Action version lookup to the assistant web_fetch capability while preserving MCP server simplicity and avoiding GitHub API rate limits.

nodes.label: RESOLVE_VERSION Placeholder Strategy
nodes.rationale: Placeholders are valid YAML, searchable, and allow progressive version resolution.

nodes.label: SHA-Pinned Workflow Actions
nodes.rationale: Commit SHA references are immutable while version comments preserve maintainability.

nodes.label: agent-shell preToolUse Policy Check
nodes.rationale: A fail-closed policy gate blocks unsafe terminal commands before an agent executes them, while fail-opening only for valid non-terminal tool input.

nodes.label: Copilot preToolUse Hook Contract
nodes.rationale: Copilot hooks communicate via JSON stdin/stdout with permissionDecision fields and must remain fast and deterministic.

nodes.label: Repository Root Discovery via git rev-parse
nodes.rationale: Repository root discovery uses git instead of cwd so hook policy paths are stable and containment checks are meaningful.
nodes.norm_label: repository root discovery via git rev-parse

nodes.label: agent-shell Flight Recorder Hooks
nodes.rationale: postToolUse is observation-only: failures should not block the agent, unlike preToolUse policy checks.

nodes.label: Agent-Shell Hook Lifecycle Configuration
nodes.rationale: init coordinates preToolUse policy and postToolUse recording so agent-shell can be configured incrementally and safely.

nodes.label: copilot-setup Agent-Shell npm Integration
nodes.rationale: npm script-shell setup remains in copilot-setup while hook-based flight recording/policy may become the superior integration path.

nodes.label: agent-shell policy --init
nodes.rationale: Static project discovery produces safe default policies; optional Copilot SDK enhancement is advisory-only to preserve deterministic baseline behavior.

nodes.label: Copilot SDK Advisory Enhancement
nodes.rationale: AI-enhanced analysis augments static discovery but falls back to static policy generation if unavailable.

nodes.label: Claude Code Web Environment Setup
nodes.rationale: Environment detection generates SessionStart hooks and merges settings/documentation so Claude Code web sessions start with required runtimes.

nodes.label: Claude SessionStart Hooks
nodes.rationale: SessionStart hooks encode detected environment setup into Claude settings while preserving existing settings/documentation.

nodes.label: CLI Project Scaffolding Init Command
nodes.rationale: CLI scaffolding reuses template-file driven filesystem structures rather than special-casing generated content.

nodes.label: citty CLI Template
nodes.rationale: Generated CLI projects should use citty and consola, matching lousy-agents itself and avoiding unnecessary database support.

nodes.label: Convert GitHub Action to JavaScript
nodes.rationale: A bundled JavaScript action can lint without npm install while preserving reviewdog inline PR reporting.

nodes.label: Reviewdog rdjsonl Reporting
nodes.rationale: Core lint output is formatted as rdjsonl and piped to reviewdog for PR annotations and check status.

nodes.label: Copilot Agent Scaffolding via CLI
nodes.rationale: Agent creation normalizes names consistently in filenames and frontmatter and refuses to overwrite existing agent files.

nodes.label: Copilot PR Review Rulesets
nodes.rationale: Rulesets target the default branch for safety and integrate Copilot review setup into the copilot-setup flow.

nodes.label: Dynamic Project-Level Templating for Webapp Scaffolding
nodes.norm_label: dynamic project-level templating for webapp scaffolding

nodes.label: Project Type Selection for Init Command
nodes.norm_label: project type selection for init command

nodes.label: MCP Server for Copilot Setup Steps Workflow Management
nodes.norm_label: mcp server for copilot setup steps workflow management

nodes.label: REST API Scaffolding for Init Command
nodes.norm_label: rest api scaffolding for init command

nodes.label: Align Webapp Init with CLI and API Templates
nodes.norm_label: align webapp init with cli and api templates

nodes.label: Include Webapp Template Files in npm Package Bundle
nodes.norm_label: include webapp template files in npm package bundle

nodes.label: Auto-Assign Copilot on Spec Issues Workflow
nodes.norm_label: auto-assign copilot on spec issues workflow

nodes.label: Copilot Setup Steps Workflow
nodes.rationale: The workflow gives Copilot coding agents a reproducible environment setup and feedback loop instead of relying on manually maintained setup instructions.

nodes.label: Common Template Nodes
nodes.rationale: Extracting shared template nodes reduces duplication and keeps CLI, REST API, and webapp scaffolding behavior consistent.

nodes.label: Severity Filtering
nodes.rationale: External config uses warn/off vocabulary while internal diagnostics use warning/error, so the adapter layer translates severities and filters disabled rules.

nodes.label: Repository Lesson Storage
nodes.rationale: Lessons are committed under .lousy-agents/lessons so Git provides the audit trail and no separate authoring API is required.

nodes.label: Lesson Glob and Path Matching Semantics
nodes.norm_label: lesson glob and path matching semantics

nodes.label: Least-Privilege Workflow Permissions
nodes.rationale: The workflow explicitly restricts default permissions and grants only contents:read for checkout.
